Below are 7 genomes from one lineage (RANDOM order). Only mechanism and niche shown.

[1] Mechanism: Hyperband allocates a fixed budget across multiple successive-halving brackets, each starting with randomly sampled hyperparameter configurations evaluated at small resource levels and repeatedly promoting only the top fraction to larger budgets, with different brackets trading off number of configurations versus training depth.
    Niche: This paper targets hyperparameter optimization when evaluating configurations is expensive and only limited computational budget is available.
[2] Mechanism: NASNet uses reinforcement-learning-based neural architecture search to discover two repeatable cell types—normal and reduction cells—modeled as small directed acyclic graphs over a restricted operation set, learned on a proxy dataset and then stacked to form deep networks for larger tasks.
    Niche: This paper targets scalable automated architecture design for large-scale image classification by learning models that can transfer from small benchmarks to larger visual recognition tasks.
[3] Mechanism: Efficient Neural Architecture Search (ENAS) uses a recurrent controller trained with policy gradients to sample architectures as subgraphs of a single over-parameterized supergraph, with all sampled models inheriting and updating shared weights instead of being trained independently.
    Niche: This paper targets the problem of efficiently searching for high-performing neural network architectures under limited computational budgets.
[4] Mechanism: DARTS performs neural architecture search by relaxing discrete operation and connection choices in a cell-based directed acyclic graph into continuous softmax-weighted mixtures and optimizing architecture parameters and network weights with bi-level gradient descent on validation and training splits respectively.
    Niche: This paper targets efficient automatic design of high-performing neural network architectures for tasks such as image classification without exhaustive manual or black-box search.
[5] Mechanism: Network In Network replaces simple linear convolutional filters with per-location micro multilayer perceptrons implemented by stacked 1×1 convolutions and nonlinearities, and uses global average pooling instead of large fully connected classifier layers.
    Niche: This paper targets image classification and visual recognition with convolutional neural networks.
[6] Mechanism: Neural Architecture Search with Reinforcement Learning uses an recurrent architecture controller policy to sequentially emit architectural decisions, trains each sampled architecture to obtain validation reward, and updates the controller with policy-gradient optimization to favor better designs.
    Niche: This paper targets the automated design of high-performing neural network architectures for a given task without manual architecture engineering.
[7] Mechanism: GoogLeNet/Inception uses repeated multi-branch modules that run 1×1, 3×3, 5×5, and pooling paths in parallel, applies 1×1 convolutions for dimensionality reduction before costly spatial filters, concatenates branch outputs, and trains with auxiliary classifiers plus global average pooling.
    Niche: This paper targets large-scale image classification and visual recognition with deep convolutional networks.

Part 1: Arrange them in correct chronological order (earliest -> latest).
  Options: M (Mutation), AR (Adaptive Radiation), H (Hybridization), S (Speciation), NC (Niche Competition)

--- Dynamics Label Definitions ---
Mutation: Direct inheritance with local modification of the driver gene.
Adaptive Radiation: The inherited mechanism is applied to a shifted problem niche.
Hybridization: The driver gene combines elements from 2+ distinct lineages.
Speciation: A new lineage-founding mechanism replaces the predecessor's approach.
Niche Competition: Papers target the same niche without inheriting the driver gene.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, use Hybridization. Speciation means the mechanism shares no ancestry with the predecessor.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

Below are 7 genomes from one lineage (RANDOM order). Only mechanism and niche shown.

[1] Mechanism: Passive dynamic walking models a two-link biped descending a shallow slope as a hybrid dynamical system with continuous gravity-driven swing/stance motion and discrete heel-strike collision maps, then analyzes periodic step cycles and their stability from the mechanism's physical parameters alone.
    Niche: This paper targets the problem of explaining and achieving stable legged walking in a minimal bipedal system.
[2] Mechanism: It introduces a hybrid feedback control framework that stabilizes periodic bipedal gaits by continuously regulating reduced-order gait variables and virtual joint constraints during swing and stance, while applying event-based corrections at foot impact and analyzing step-to-step stability with return maps.
    Niche: This paper targets the problem of achieving robust, repeatable dynamic walking in underactuated bipedal robots subject to impacts and phase-dependent locomotion dynamics.
[3] Mechanism: A quadruped system is built around an actuator with switchable transmission that actively changes gear ratio between high-torque stance mode and low-impedance, high-speed swing mode, combined with proximal actuation, lightweight compliant legs, and morphology tuned to spring-mass running templates.
    Niche: This paper targets the design of a quadrupedal robot capable of highly dynamic running, bounding, and galloping with both strong stance support and rapid leg swing.
[4] Mechanism: The method uses a hierarchical whole-body MPC architecture that optimizes reduced-order centroidal dynamics and contact forces over a receding horizon, then tracks the resulting plan with full-body inverse-dynamics torque control under friction, support, and actuator constraints.
    Niche: This paper targets real-time whole-body locomotion and manipulation for a torque-controlled humanoid robot operating in cluttered, uneven, and only partially modeled environments.
[5] Mechanism: Hybrid zero-dynamics embedding: the method imposes feedback-enforced virtual constraints on an asymmetric hopper and constructs an impact-invariant reduced manifold so the closed-loop hybrid system evolves exactly as a Spring Loaded Inverted Pendulum template.
    Niche: This paper targets explaining and realizing low-dimensional hopping locomotion behavior within a more detailed asymmetric legged mechanism across stance and flight phases.
[6] Mechanism: A balance-centered feedback control method uses simplified whole-body dynamics to regulate center-of-mass motion, body orientation, and ground reaction/contact forces across hybrid support phases instead of tracking fully precomputed gait trajectories.
    Niche: This paper targets the problem of maintaining upright balance and disturbance-robust locomotion in articulated legged robots during standing and walking.
[7] Mechanism: A sim-to-real reinforcement learning method trains a closed-loop policy entirely in simulation to map onboard state observations directly to low-level motor commands while using dynamics randomization, sensor/actuator noise injection, and reward shaping to induce transfer-robust locomotion.
    Niche: This paper targets robust real-world quadruped locomotion that can be deployed directly on physical robots for stable, disturbance-tolerant movement.

Below are 7 genomes from one lineage (RANDOM order). Only mechanism and niche shown.

[1] Mechanism: It uses a one-class kernel support estimation method that maps data into a reproducing kernel feature space and solves a regularized convex optimization problem to find a decision boundary enclosing most observations while permitting a specified fraction to lie outside as outliers.
    Niche: The paper targets unsupervised anomaly or novelty detection by estimating the region of feature space occupied by normal data in the presence of contamination.
[2] Mechanism: Variational Autoencoder (Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes): jointly train an encoder network that amortizes variational posterior inference and a decoder generative network by maximizing the ELBO, using the reparameterization trick to express latent sampling as a differentiable transformation of fixed noise.
    Niche: This paper targets scalable learning and inference for continuous latent-variable probabilistic generative models on large datasets.
[3] Mechanism: Isolation Forest detects anomalies by building an ensemble of random binary partition trees from small subsamples, where each split picks a feature and threshold uniformly at random, and scoring points by their normalized average root-to-leaf path length.
    Niche: This paper targets unsupervised anomaly detection in large, high-dimensional datasets.
[4] Mechanism: It introduces the one-class SVM, which maps data into an RKHS with a positive definite kernel and solves a convex maximum-margin optimization that separates the samples from the origin with slack controlled by a parameter nu to estimate a high-density support region.
    Niche: The paper targets unsupervised estimation of the support of a high-dimensional probability distribution, i.e. finding a compact region containing most normal data while rejecting outliers.
[5] Mechanism: AnoGAN learns a Model-GAN on normal data only and, at inference, iteratively optimizes a latent code for each query so that the generated sample matches it, then scores anomaly as a weighted combination of input-space residual error and discriminator feature-space discrepancy.
    Niche: This paper targets unsupervised detection of abnormal samples and localization of atypical regions in domains where training data are mostly normal, to support downstream marker discovery.
[6] Mechanism: It applies a one-class SVM to sparse text features, learning a maximum-margin boundary around positive documents by separating them from the origin while permitting a controlled fraction of outliers via regularization.
    Niche: This paper targets document classification settings where only examples of the target category are available and negative documents are scarce, noisy, or ill-defined.
[7] Mechanism: Generative Adversarial Networks train a generator that maps simple random noise to data samples and a discriminator that classifies real versus generated inputs, with both optimized jointly through a minimax adversarial objective so the generator implicitly matches the data distribution without explicit likelihoods.
    Niche: This paper targets unsupervised generative modeling of complex data distributions, especially learning to synthesize realistic samples from high-dimensional data such as images.

[1] Mechanism: It uses a systematic empirical workload characterization methodology that implements representative kernels and applications in CUDA and analyzes their behavior under the GPU execution model, including thread grouping, shared memory use, memory access regularity, branch divergence, latency hiding, and host-device transfer costs.
    Niche: This paper targets understanding which classes of general-purpose applications achieve high performance on CUDA-programmed GPUs and which do not.
[2] Mechanism: Fermi is a many-core CUDA architecture that scales streaming multiprocessors while adding coherent hardware-managed caches, configurable on-chip local memory, improved warp scheduling, stronger atomics and concurrency, ECC memory, and much higher double-precision capability.
    Niche: This paper targets making GPUs broadly effective for general-purpose parallel computing workloads, especially irregular, memory-intensive, and high-performance scientific applications.
[3] Mechanism: The Kepler GK110 architecture redesigns the streaming multiprocessor and memory subsystem by increasing warp residency, instruction issue width, register capacity, double-precision and atomic throughput, concurrent kernel execution, and cache/shared-memory flexibility to raise delivered performance per watt and programmability.
    Niche: This paper targets high-performance, throughput-oriented computing for both graphics workloads and general-purpose scientific and parallel applications on an accelerator.
[4] Mechanism: CUDA is a GPU programming system that exposes a hierarchy of kernels, threads, thread blocks, grids, and distinct memory spaces so programmers can write data-parallel code whose blocks are dynamically scheduled across multiprocessors and whose cooperating threads use fast shared memory with local synchronization.
    Niche: This paper targets general-purpose high-throughput computation on graphics processors across a broad range of non-graphics workloads.
[5] Mechanism: Brook for GPUs introduces a stream programming system in which programmers write kernels over stream elements while a compiler and runtime map streams to textures and kernels to fragment programs, managing execution through the graphics pipeline.
    Niche: This paper targets making general-purpose data-parallel computation on graphics processors practical and accessible for non-graphics applications.
[6] Mechanism: It uses a reverse-engineering microbenchmarking methodology in which many tightly controlled kernels and instruction-level timing probes independently stress specific Volta subsystems—pipelines, caches, shared memory, registers, schedulers, translation paths, synchronization, and tensor units—to infer undocumented latency, throughput, granularity, and contention properties.
    Niche: This paper targets the problem of obtaining an accurate, programmer-relevant characterization of the NVIDIA Volta GPU's real microarchitectural behavior and performance limits.
[7] Mechanism: The Volta V100 architecture introduces Tensor Cores inside each SM to execute mixed-precision matrix fused multiply-add operations at very high throughput, alongside a redesigned SM, cache/memory hierarchy, and high-bandwidth HBM2 memory system to keep those units fed efficiently.
    Niche: This paper targets data-center acceleration for both high-performance scientific computing and deep learning training/inference workloads.

    Niche: The paper targets integrating data from multiple heterogeneous sources by deciding which records, fields, and schema mappings correspond across sources.
[3] Mechanism: TSIMMIS uses a mediator-wrapper architecture in which source-specific wrappers convert diverse source formats and access methods into a common self-describing object-like representation, and mediators then restructure, combine, and filter that representation to answer cross-source queries.

    Niche: This paper targets organization, discovery, understanding, and governance of large numbers of heterogeneous internal datasets in a rapidly growing enterprise data ecosystem.

[1] Mechanism: It introduces a neural multi-instance relation extraction model that encodes each sentence with word and entity-position features, then applies relation-conditioned selective attention mechanism to weight and sum sentence representations within an entity-pair bag for bag-level classification.
    Niche: This paper targets distantly supervised relation extraction from text, especially the problem of predicting a relation for an entity pair when many aligned sentences are noisy or do not express the labeled relation.
[2] Mechanism: It uses a position-aware attention mechanism neural model that builds sentence representations from word embeddings while computing attention mechanism weights from both token semantics and each token’s relative distance to the query entities, trained with task-specific supervised slot-filling data.
    Niche: This paper targets relation extraction for knowledge-base slot filling, where the goal is to identify the correct filler for a query entity-slot pair from text.
[3] Mechanism: It uses a pipeline-oriented KBP system that decomposes the task into document retrieval, entity and mention detection, coreference, slot filling, temporal reasoning, and confidence calibration, with weakly supervised relation extraction via aligning existing KB facts to text and schema-driven filtering and evidence aggregation across documents.
    Niche: This paper targets end-to-end knowledge base population from large, heterogeneous text collections by constructing entity entries with attributes and relations.
[4] Mechanism: The shortest path dependency kernel computes similarity between candidate entity pairs by extracting the shortest dependency path between them in a parsed sentence and applying a structured kernel over the sequence of path nodes and dependency edges annotated with lexical, POS, and dependency-label information.
    Niche: This paper targets sentence-level semantic relation extraction between pairs of entities mentioned in text.
[5] Mechanism: Distant supervision trains a multi-class relation classifier by aligning known entity-relation facts from a structured knowledge base with sentences in an unlabeled corpus that mention the corresponding entity pairs, using those co-occurrences as noisy positive and null-labeled examples with lexical and syntactic features.
    Niche: This paper targets relation extraction from text without requiring manually labeled training sentences.
[6] Mechanism: A multi-knowledge feature-based relation extraction method builds separate representations from lexical context, shallow syntactic cues, dependency structure, and relational links between arguments, then evaluates classifiers using each source alone and in combination.
    Niche: This paper targets supervised classification of semantic relations between entity mentions in text.
[7] Mechanism: It uses high-precision lexico-syntactic pattern matching over shallowly parsed text to detect noun phrases in diagnostic constructions like 'such as,' 'including,' appositives, and enumerations, then converts those contexts into candidate hyponym–hypernym pairs.
    Niche: The paper targets automatic acquisition of hyponym–hypernym relations for building lexical taxonomies from unrestricted large text corpora.

    Niche: This paper targets network intrusion detection under realistic open-world conditions where future attack types may differ from those seen during training.
[7] Mechanism: It implements a deep learning–based network traffic classifier over streamlined flow features within a continuous monitoring pipeline whose predictions are directly mapped to automated prevention actions such as blocking or filtering suspicious traffic.
    Niche: This paper targets real-time network attack detection and immediate intrusion prevention in live traffic environments under strict latency constraints.

  Options: M (Mutation), AR (Adaptive Radiation), H (Hybridization), S (Speciation), NC (Niche Competition)

--- Dynamics Label Definitions ---
Mutation: Direct inheritance with local modification of the driver gene.
Adaptive Radiation: The inherited mechanism is applied to a shifted problem niche.
Hybridization: The driver gene combines elements from 2+ distinct lineages.
Speciation: A new lineage-founding mechanism replaces the predecessor's approach.
Niche Competition: Papers target the same niche without inheriting the driver gene.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, use Hybridization. Speciation means the mechanism shares no ancestry with the predecessor.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.
